name: Geant4
description: A C++ toolkit for simulating the passage of particles through matter. Developed at CERN and used for detector design, medical physics, and space radiation studies.
whyUseIt: Geant4 simulates how particles (photons, electrons, protons, neutrons, etc.) interact with materials — scattering, absorption, pair production, nuclear reactions. It is essential for designing particle detectors, radiation shielding, and medical imaging systems.
fieldPosition: Particle physics, medical physics, space science — any field involving radiation-matter interaction
physicistWorkflow: Define detector geometry and materials, specify the particle source, run the simulation, analyse energy deposits and particle tracks.
supervisorExpects: Ability to set up and validate Geant4 simulations for detector design or radiation transport studies.
computationalImpact: Enables virtual prototyping of detectors — test designs computationally before building expensive hardware.

vocabulary.definition: The set of physics models used to simulate particle interactions
A detector volume that records energy deposits and particle information

beginnerMistakes: Using inappropriate physics models for the energy range of interest
Not validating simulation results against experimental data
